interesting_strings: For my first trick, I will attempt to fit 56 bytes of user input into 32 bytes of stack buffer!
You there, may I have your input please? And don't worry about null bytes, we're using read()!

suspected_vulns.evidence: printf-like sink imported; verify user-controlled format paths.

recommended_methods: try payloads with and without a stack-alignment ret gadget
prompt_warnings: This is a ROP-style challenge; a mere input string is not enough.
recommended_local_tools: gadget_search(pop rdi / ret / leave / ret as needed)
recommended_preruns: capture startup output before guessing interaction
runtime_hints: This challenge may depend on sidecar shared libraries or helper files in the runtime directory.
Look for flag.txt or print_file-related file access; the target may expect a runtime cwd with helper files.
